ibd_diagnosis_date_endoscopy.description: What was the date of IBD diagnosis confirmed by endoscopy? Use YYYY-MM-DD format in your response. Annotations MUST include day, month, and year (e.g. 01/15/2021). If you have Month or Year level precision, your annotation should take the form of the first possible date consistent with that annotation.
ibd_diagnosis_date_endoscopy.title: Ibd Diagnosis Date Endoscopy

ibd_diagnosis_date.description: What was the date of IBD diagnosis? Use YYYY-MM-DD format in your response. Annotations MUST include day, month, and year (e.g. 01/15/2021). If you have Month or Year level precision, your annotation should take the form of the first possible date consistent with that annotation.
ibd_diagnosis_date.title: Ibd Diagnosis Date

IBDActivity.description:
Current documented IBD disease activity.

Use ACTIVE when the note documents current active disease, flare,
ongoing inflammation, or active symptoms attributable to IBD.

Use REMISSION when the note documents remission, quiescent disease,
inactive disease, no active disease, or asymptomatic/inactive IBD as
the current state.
IBDActivity.title: IBDActivity

IBDActivityAndSeverityMention.description:
Current documented IBD disease activity and severity.

Extract current disease activity separately from severity.

Examples:
* "IBD in remission" -> activity=REMISSION, severity=NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE
* "active Crohn's disease" -> activity=ACTIVE, severity=NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE
* "mild disease" -> activity=ACTIVE, severity=MILD
* "mild to moderate disease" -> activity=ACTIVE, severity=MILD_TO_MODERATE
* "moderate disease" -> activity=ACTIVE, severity=MODERATE
* "moderate to severe disease" -> activity=ACTIVE, severity=MODERATE_TO_SEVERE
* "severe disease" -> activity=ACTIVE, severity=SEVERE

Preserve the exact supporting phrase in spans.

If a severity term is negated, such as "no severe disease", do not assign
that severity level unless another non-negated severity is documented.

If multiple severities are documented for different anatomic segments,
such as "severe proctitis with mild left-sided involvement", use the
highest documented severity.

Do not infer severity from medication intensity alone.
Do not use historical severity if the note says the patient is now in remission.
Do not classify family history as patient disease activity or severity.

has_mention.description: Indicates whether the concept was mentioned in the text.
has_mention.title: Has Mention

spans.description: The verbatim text where this concept was mentioned.
spans.title: Spans

ibd_activity.description: Current documented IBD disease activity. ACTIVE: current active disease, active inflammation, active flare, currently flaring, or active IBD symptoms are documented and attributed to IBD; REMISSION: remission, quiescent disease, inactive disease, no active disease, or asymptomatic/inactive IBD is documented as the current state. NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE: current IBD activity is not documented.

ibd_severity.description: Current documented IBD disease severity. Use the enum value that best matches the clinician's wording. MILD: phrases such as 'mild disease' or 'mild activity'. MILD_TO_MODERATE: phrases such as 'mild to moderate disease'. MODERATE: phrases such as 'moderate disease' or 'moderate activity'. MODERATE_TO_SEVERE: phrases such as 'moderate to severe disease'. SEVERE: phrases such as 'severe disease' or 'severe activity'. NONE_OF_THE_ABOVE: severity is not documented, or only activity is documented without a severity grade, such as 'active disease' or 'active flare'.

IBDActivityAndSeverityMention.title: IBDActivityAndSeverityMention

IBDSeverity.description:
Current documented IBD disease severity.

These values intentionally include common clinician range phrases because
LLM extraction quality is usually better when enum options closely match
the language used in clinical notes.
IBDSeverity.title: IBDSeverity

enum: None of the above (e.g. not mentioned, patient does not have IBD, etc)

ibd_type.description: Which subtype of inflammatory bowel disease (IBD) is documented for this patient?

description:
An object-model for annotations of inflammatory bowel disorder observations
found in a patient's chart, related to initial diagnosis and current disease status.
Take care to avoid false positives, like confusing information that only
appears in family history for patient history. Annotations should indicate
the relevant details of the finding, as well as some additional evidence
metadata to validate findings post-hoc.
title: IbdDiagnosisAnnotation
